description_long: This object is a large, roughly triangular loaf of rustic bread with a thick, dense crust. The surface is matte and lightly textured, showing natural cracks and irregularities typical of hand-formed, artisanal bread. One side of the loaf features handwritten text, possibly for identification or cataloging, which is unusual for bread in an urban context. The color is a consistent light tan to beige, with subtle variations and some darker marks, suggesting a well-baked exterior. The loaf appears unsliced and solid, with no visible openings or receptacle features. Its overall form is chunky and substantial, resembling a traditional country loaf or a large piece of flatbread.
description: A large, rustic, unsliced loaf of bread with a thick, matte, tan crust and handwritten markings on one side.
description_view_0: A broad, flat face of the bread with a natural, slightly rough surface and no visible markings.
description_view_1: A narrow edge view showing the thickness and dense structure of the loaf, with some surface irregularities.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face, featuring handwritten text and a slightly smoother surface compared to the other side.
description_view_3: Another narrow edge, similar to view 1, highlighting the loaf's thickness and natural crust texture.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust is thick and matte with a natural, irregular texture, featuring some cracks and subtle surface marks. There are no visible scores, seeds, or flour dusting, giving it a rustic, hand-formed appearance.
